verses.text: David resolves to praise the LORD with his whole heart, telling all His marvellous works, glad and rejoicing in His name. Wholehearted praise is his aim. The mercies of God deserve not a divided but an undivided heart.

David resolves to praise the LORD with his whole heart, telling all His marvellous works, glad and rejoicing in His name. Wholehearted praise is his aim. The mercies of God deserve not a divided but an undivided heart.
His enemies turned back and perished at God's presence, their cities and memory blotted out. The Judge has acted. When God arises, the proud foe is undone and even his name forgotten.
His enemies turned back and perished at God's presence, their cities and memory blotted out. The Judge has acted. When God arises, the proud foe is undone and even his name forgotten.
His enemies turned back and perished at God's presence, their cities and memory blotted out. The Judge has acted. When God arises, the proud foe is undone and even his name forgotten.
His enemies turned back and perished at God's presence, their cities and memory blotted out. The Judge has acted. When God arises, the proud foe is undone and even his name forgotten.

The LORD endures forever and will judge the world in righteousness, becoming 'a refuge for the oppressed,' known to all who seek Him. His throne is sure and just. Those who know His name learn He never forsakes the trusting.

The LORD endures forever and will judge the world in righteousness, becoming 'a refuge for the oppressed,' known to all who seek Him. His throne is sure and just. Those who know His name learn He never forsakes the trusting.

The LORD endures forever and will judge the world in righteousness, becoming 'a refuge for the oppressed,' known to all who seek Him. His throne is sure and just. Those who know His name learn He never forsakes the trusting.

The LORD endures forever and will judge the world in righteousness, becoming 'a refuge for the oppressed,' known to all who seek Him. His throne is sure and just. Those who know His name learn He never forsakes the trusting.

Sing praises to the LORD who dwells in Zion, for He remembers the blood of the afflicted and does not forget the cry of the humble. He keeps account of every wrong. The lowly may be ignored by men, but never by God.

Sing praises to the LORD who dwells in Zion, for He remembers the blood of the afflicted and does not forget the cry of the humble. He keeps account of every wrong. The lowly may be ignored by men, but never by God.

David asks God to see his trouble and lift him from the gates of death, that he may praise Him in the gates of Zion. He prays to be rescued for worship's sake. Deliverance is sought not for ease alone but to declare God's praise.

David asks God to see his trouble and lift him from the gates of death, that he may praise Him in the gates of Zion. He prays to be rescued for worship's sake. Deliverance is sought not for ease alone but to declare God's praise.

The heathen sink in the pit they dug, caught in their own net, for 'the LORD is known by the judgment which he executeth.' Their schemes recoil on them. God reveals Himself in the just downfall of the wicked.

The heathen sink in the pit they dug, caught in their own net, for 'the LORD is known by the judgment which he executeth.' Their schemes recoil on them. God reveals Himself in the just downfall of the wicked.
The wicked who forget God shall be turned into hell, but 'the needy shall not alway be forgotten.' Two destinies are set. God's seeming delay is not denial; the hope of the poor will not perish.
The wicked who forget God shall be turned into hell, but 'the needy shall not alway be forgotten.' Two destinies are set. God's seeming delay is not denial; the hope of the poor will not perish.

'Arise, O LORD,' David prays, 'let the nations be judged' and 'know themselves to be but men.' He longs for God to humble human pride. The proud need only to remember they are mortal before the eternal God.

'Arise, O LORD,' David prays, 'let the nations be judged' and 'know themselves to be but men.' He longs for God to humble human pride. The proud need only to remember they are mortal before the eternal God.
